text.title: django-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Five Django API antipatterns the spec must forbid.

antipattern.symptom: View's `post()` runs 80 lines: validation, balance check, email send, audit log, DB writes. Test setup requires HTTP fixtures + mocked email. Reusing logic from a Celery task means duplicating it.
antipattern.root-cause: Business logic ended up in the view instead of a service function. Easy to write in the moment; impossible to test cleanly.
antipattern.fix: Move business logic to `<app>/services.py` with explicit dependencies. The view becomes: parse → call service → return. Spec rule r2-thin-views forbids fat views.

antipattern.symptom: Test setup requires constructing a fake DRF request to test "can the user place this order?". Business validation depends on serializer state.
antipattern.root-cause: validate_* methods on serializers contain balance checks, ownership checks, or workflow guards. The serializer's job is shape + type validation; business rules belong elsewhere.
antipattern.fix: Strip validate_* down to shape/type. Move business validation to the service function, where it can be tested with plain Python args.

antipattern.symptom: Migration adds an internal `password_hash` field to a User-like model. Next day, the field appears in the public API response.
antipattern.root-cause: `Meta.fields = "__all__"` on the serializer auto-exposes every model field. There is no compile-time check; only a security review can catch it.
antipattern.fix: Replace `__all__` with an explicit field list. Use `read_only_fields` to lock id/uid/timestamps. Spec rule r4-no-all-fields is checked by the validator.

antipattern.symptom: Production API responds with HTML when called with `Accept: */*` from a misconfigured client. CSRF errors appear sporadically.
antipattern.root-cause: BrowsableAPIRenderer is in DEFAULT_RENDERER_CLASSES on production settings. The HTML browsable API changes content negotiation and exposes API structure.
antipattern.fix: Production renderers = [JSONRenderer]. Browsable API is dev-only (settings.dev splits the renderer list). Spec consumer's drf-settings.py template forbids BrowsableAPIRenderer in prod.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint declared `async def`. Tests pass under WSGI dev server. Under ASGI in production, latency under load doubles instead of dropping.
antipattern.root-cause: Plain DRF dispatches handlers synchronously. An `async def` method on a DRF view is awaited by Django but the surrounding pipeline still blocks the event loop.
antipattern.fix: Either: use `adrf` (Async DRF) which adds an async dispatch path, OR switch the resource to Ninja, which is async-native. The spec's framework field locks the choice; mixed sync/async DRF is forbidden.
